name: ram_bw_copy
title: Memory copy bandwidth
short_description: Sustained bandwidth for copying data within main memory.

structured_text.description: Copies large arrays from one region of main memory to another across all cores, reading and writing at the same time over a working set far larger than the caches. This combined pattern is how most real data movement behaves, since data is usually read, transformed and written back.
structured_text.metric_explanation: Bandwidth is the number of bytes moved per second counting both the read and the write side, reported in GB/s.
structured_text.value_interpretation: Higher is better. Copy bandwidth reflects everyday data movement more closely than read or write alone, since both happen together.
structured_text.reasoning: Real workloads rarely only read or only write, so a copy test that does both at once is the most representative single measure of memory throughput.

domain_gaming.explanation: Moving and transforming world and texture data combines reads and writes each frame.

domain_office.explanation: Office work moves modest amounts of data through memory.

domain_software_development.explanation: Builds move and transform data structures, mixing reads and writes.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Editing reads image buffers, transforms them and writes them back.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Processing frames reads and writes high resolution buffers together.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: Moving scene and buffer data combines reads and writes during rendering.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Audio processing reads buffers and writes results in the same pass.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: Solvers stream data in and out of memory while iterating.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: Stencil and update kernels read and write large arrays together, making copy bandwidth decisive.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Shaping and moving training data mixes reads and writes through memory.

domain_ai_inference.explanation: Reading weights and writing activations together is copy heavy work.

domain_data_science.explanation: Transforming wide tables reads input and writes output in the same pass.

domain_server_virt.explanation: Many processes moving data at once share the same copy bandwidth.
